finding.summary: Backing vocal stack shows an unusually bass-heavy 1/3-octave profile: the 100 Hz band is 6 dB above 1 kHz and 12 dB above 4 kHz. Dynamics, level, and stereo image are otherwise healthy, but the fx_chain is empty so there is no EQ available on this track to investigate or tame that region.
finding.probable_cause: The 100 Hz-to-1 kHz tilt (+6 dB) is inverted from what vocal formants typically produce, where 1 kHz-region energy usually exceeds sub-200 Hz energy. Likely contributors are proximity effect from close mic'ing, low-frequency room rumble or mic-stand vibration, or the natural low fundamentals of a bass-dominant stack. With no FX in fx_chain, there is no high-pass or EQ to either confirm the cause (by bypass/A/B) or address it.
finding.confidence_reason: audio.silence_fraction is 0.05, so the spectrum and level measurements are reliable, and capture isolation is verified. Confidence is not higher because a coarse 3-band 1/3-octave snapshot cannot separate wanted low-end body from unwanted rumble, and with an empty fx_chain there is no bypassable processor to use as a diagnostic A/B.

evidence_refs.description: 1 kHz at -24.0 dBFS - 6 dB below 100 Hz
4 kHz at -30.0 dBFS - 12 dB below 100 Hz
0.05 - low, so spectral/loudness stats are not diluted by silence
Empty array - no EQ, HPF, or compressor available to act on
-20.0 LUFS - appropriate level for a BGV stack
-5.5 dBTP - safe headroom under streaming targets
0.77 - healthy positive correlation, no phase issue

proposal.reason: The spectral evidence points toward trying a gentle high-pass near 100-150 Hz, but the fx_chain is empty and the supported operations (set_fx_param, set_fx_bypass) require an existing plugin instance. Track volume at 0.0 dB already lands the track at a sensible -20 LUFS for a BGV stack, pan is centered, and there is nothing to bypass. Making a move I cannot verify against this single-track evidence would be fabrication. Next step outside this tool: insert a high-pass EQ around 100-150 Hz with a gentle slope on this track (or on its bus), then re-capture and compare spectrum_third_octave[0].level_db against this snapshot.
